Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Continued To Jan-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard:
PTR-23-305978 The Leonard Allen Hatley and Dorothy C. Hatley Irrevocable Trust, Dated 11/13/90; and
PTR-23-305982 The Leonard Allen Hatley and Dorothy C. Hatley 1985 Trust Dated 8/4/85.

Appearances:
Ann Kavanagh for Mary Meyer, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Kevin Rodriguez for Abigail Hatley, Beneficiary (via Zoom).
John Stein for James Hatley, Trustee (via Zoom).

Discussions are held as to a mediation scheduled for two days in December. Mr. Stein argued that suspending the trustee before mediation would harm the trust, disrupt ongoing real estate transactions, and negatively affect mediation outcomes. Mr. Rodrigues expressed concerns about the trustee withholding distributions.

The court declined to suspend the trustee before mediation and suggested that all issues should be addressed at mediation and that suspension beforehand could be counterproductive. The court sets a status hearing for January 7, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, as to the outcome of mediation. Parties are to file a joint status report by December 31, 2024.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-23-305978 The Leonard Allen Hatley and Dorothy C. Hatley 1985 Trust and PTR-23-305978 The Leonard Allen Hatley and Dorothy C. Hatley Irrevocable Trust.

Appearances: Ann Kavanagh for Mary Meyer (petitioner/beneficiary).
John Stein for James Hatley (trustee).
Stacie Nelson appeared by zoom for Teresa Hatley (beneficiary).
Kevin Rodriguez appeared by zoom for Abigail Hatley (beneficiary).

Discussion was held regarding the issue of discovery not having commenced, the trial being a long one and needing to go out to Dept. 206, the parties not being ready to set the matter for trial, suspension of the trustee, the parties not having received information regarding the tax returns or the assets of the LLC, there being a fire at one property and said property having to be held for a year or two, Mr. Rodriguez's clients wanting to see the trust distributed and get everything liquidated, the court issuing an order to mediation in 120-days and distribution of the ILIT asset, a request that information be provided at least 30-days prior to mediation, the life insurance trust being distributed with a small reserve, having a planning process in place, the capital gains tax issue, making distribution in kind, etc. The parties are ordered to meet and confer either in person or by zoom by July 10, 2024. The Petitions for Order Suspending Trustee and Appointing Temporary Trustee, etc., (2), and the status conference are continued to July 30, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court's hope is to set a mediation schedule at that time. The parties are to file a joint status report by July 17, 2024 describing what information is needed in order to go to mediation and talk about the ILIT asset.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR # 5229, 415-828-1919, peggytsujimotogmail.com

Petition For Order: (1) Suspending Powers Of Trustee, James L. S. Hatley, Pending Hearing On Petition For Removal And Appointing A Temporary Trustee; (2) Instructing Temporary Trustee To Appraise Certain Trust Assets; (3) Instructing Temporary Trustee Regarding Certain Trust Assets; (4) Compelling Trustee, James L. S. Hatley, And Appointing A Successor Trustee; And (6) Instructing The Successor Trustee To Terminate The Trusts And Distribute The Trusts' Assets
Off Calendar Mar-06-2024 Continued To Jun-12-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Ann Kavanagh, Esq., for Mary Meyer, Petitioner.
JOHN J. STEIN, Esq., for James L.S. Hatley, Respondent.
Kevin Rodriguez, Esq., for Abigail Hatley, Beneficiary.
Stacie Nelson, Esq., for Teresa Hatley, Beneficiary.

At the outset, the court queries counsel regarding where trust administration takes place. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the court's query. At conclusion of argument, the court waives the venue issue. At the court's query, Ms. Kavanagh then offers an update regarding mediation efforts stating that her clients are amenable to mediation but that there needs to be valuation of the assets before there can be meaningful settlement discussion and distributions. Ms. Kavanagh believes that scheduling an evidentiary hearing to help with the preceding. At the court's query, Mr. Stein states that an evidentiary hearing may be beneficial if the parties are unable to resolve the above on their own. The court states that it doesn't believe that this matter is currently ready to schedule an evidentiary hearing and encourages the parties to meet and confer. The court confirms that it has the capability to accommodate a certain amount of time for an evidentiary hearing and if the parties require additional time, this court will send the matter to Dept. 206 for trial assignment. Ms. Nelson requests the court to suspend the Trustee. The court advises that it is unable to do so but invites counsel to brief the request. After additional discussion, the court advises that it intends to continue this matter to a future date.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Jun-12-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. A Status Conference is added to the same date. Counsel is instructed to file reports on May-29-2024. The court asks counsel to include an estimated length of an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Nelson is invited to include her request for suspension within her brief.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
